name: Oper Frankfurt
notes:
Live: /de/spielplan/ (var dates_available spine + repertoire-element cards; ?datum=YYYY-MM-DD pages the season). Per-production detail page /de/spielplan/{slug}/ is authoritative: h2 title, h4 composer, creative team as a clean
under 'Besetzung', sung cast in panel-body

blocks, and a 'calendar-list' of every performance date grouped by month. NO deep archive — /de/spielplan/archiv/ is gone (404); the site exposes only the current rolling ~13-month repertoire, so pre-season backfill must come from Wikidata. Adapter implemented (future leg complete).

name: Staatsoper Unter den Linden
notes: Adapter implemented (future + archive). Server-rendered: /de/spielplan/{DD-MM-YYYY}/ lists termin items (full <time datetime> + link to /de/veranstaltungen/{slug}.{id}/); page is a forward window, step the date to walk the season. Past seasons at /de/spielplan/archiv/{YYYY-YYYY}/. Production page: h1 title, 'Musik von …' composer, besetzung__item list (creative + sung cast, German labels).

name: Deutsche Oper Berlin
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). JS calendar over /de_DE/event.json (paginated ?date=DD.MM.YYYY&p=N) — each event has Title, IdEventCluster (production key), DateDay/Month/Year + DateTime, DetailLink. Group by cluster; detail page gives composer (event_detail_headline 'Title Composer [dates]') + cast/creative (<li><div class=role> + person-link/person-name, German labels). Future-only → deep history via Wikidata. Endpoint found via headless-browser network capture.

name: Semperoper Dresden
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Server-rendered: /spielplan.html lists ni-schedule-event rows (category+title+composer, date 'D. Monat YYYY, HH Uhr', ni-event-venue, link /spielplan/stuecke/stid/{slug}/{id}.html). Group rows by production id; detail page gives h1 title + cast/creative (ni-cast-item-label + ni-cast-item-link, German labels). Schedule is a forward window → deep history via Wikidata. TODO: paginate the schedule for the full season.

name: Staatsoper Stuttgart
notes: Adapter implemented (spielplan-html + wikidata backfill). Looks like an SPA but is server-rendered (JS only rehydrates) → plain fetch, no browser. /spielplan/kalender/ lists /spielplan/kalender/{slug}/{id}/ links (one per night); each detail page's <title> = 'Title, von Composer - DD.MM.YYYY, HH:MM | …' and <meta name=description> carries the cast+creative as one 'Rolle: Name, …, Musikalische Leitung: Name, …' run. Fetch every performance page (its own date), group by slug.

name: Oper Köln
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Nuxt front end over a Django-REST API: /de/api/events/ (DRF page-paginated, ?page=N) returns every announced performance today-forward with production id, writer (composer), start, stage_obj, sold-out/cancelled flags, and cast_objs/productionteam_objs (role_obj.name + person_obj.name, German labels). Group events by production. API is future-only → deep history via Wikidata.

name: Staatsoper Hamburg
notes: Adapter implemented (spielplan-html + wikidata backfill). Server-rendered on the new domain die-hamburgische-staatsoper.de. Dates from the full calendar /de/kalender (one <li class=event-entry> per night, data-href=/de/programm/{genre}/{id}-{slug} + event__date 'Fr 5.6.26' + time + location); grouped by production. Detail page per production: h1 title, 'Komposition: X' composer, production-infos__item list (label + person, creative + sung cast, German labels). Future-only → Wikidata backfill.

name: Komische Oper Berlin
notes: Adapter implemented (spielplan-html + wikidata backfill). Currently at Schillertheater (Stammhaus renovation). Default /spielplan/ server-renders the upcoming window: performance blocks have data-return-point='{date}-p{id}' + link /spielplan/kalender/{slug}/{id}/ (month pages are JS-paginated, so window-limited for now). Detail page <meta name=description> = 'Title, Composer, … Besetzung: Role: Name, …, Musikalische Leitung: Name, …' → composer = 2nd field, cast+creative = colon-comma run. Title h1 has soft-hyphens.

name: Deutsche Oper am Rhein
notes: Same shared German-theatre CMS as Komische Oper (houses/_theater-cms.ts): default /spielplan/ server-renders blocks with data-return-point='{date}-p{id}' + /spielplan/kalender/{slug}/{id}/ links; detail <meta description> = 'Title, Composer, … Besetzung: …'. Company spans Düsseldorf + Duisburg.

name: Nationaltheater Mannheim
notes: Shared German-theatre CMS (houses/_theater-cms.ts), no-'kalender/' link variant (/spielplan/{slug}/{id}/) and 'von Composer' / 'Abendbesetzung:' meta dialect. data-return-point dates on /spielplan/.

name: Oper Leipzig
notes:
Nuxt but server-rendered. /de/programm lists /de/programm/{slug}/{id} links. Detail page: composer =

before the

title (infos-title); creative+cast =
  • Label … Name; performances = event-item__subline--date spans ('Wd. DD.MM.YYYY | HH:MM | Venue'). Future-only → Wikidata backfill.

name: Staatsoper Hannover
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Multi-genre Staatstheater on the same cb-event framework as Deutsche Oper Berlin: the calendar reads /de_DE/event.json (paginated ?p=N, filtered from date_from; full param set current_language/keyword/status/location=alle/category=alle/date_from/q/query). Each row has IdEventCluster (production key), Title, ContentCategory (filter to Oper/Musiktheater), OpusInfo ('… von Composer' line), and a structured Ensemble (creative + sung cast, German labels) inline — richer than DOB, no detail fetch needed for credits. But every row of a cluster repeats the run-start date, so individual performance dates come from the detail page /de_DE/programm/{slug}.{id} ('further-event' articles, aria-label 'Weekday, DD. Monat YYYY, HH:MM …'). Future-only → Wikidata backfill. QID was wrong (Q321699 = a French commune); corrected to Q315705 (Opernhaus Hannover building, has the premiere-location link).

name: Theater Bremen
notes:
Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). A cb-event house whose /de_DE/event.json 500s for external clients, but the season index /de_DE/programm-next is fully server-rendered: one '.productions' block per show (h1 accordeon-title link + '

' opening with the genre line 'Oper … von Composer'). Enumerate the opera blocks there (OPERA_GENRE). Each detail page /de_DE/programm/{slug}.{id} carries the full Besetzung ('Label []Name') and all performance dates in an 'events-tickets' block ('Weekday, DD. Monat YYYY, HH:MM Uhr',
-separated). Canonical host has NO www. Future-only → Wikidata backfill.

name: Theater Bonn
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Django/Vue SPA over a REST API (base /de/api). /de/api/repertoire/ lists productions with a 'categories' array (filter to 'Oper') and a 'description' whose first is the composer, followed by 'Label: Name' creative pairs. /de/api/events/ lists every performance with the production 'url', date_full (DD.MM.YYYY) and date_time ('HH:MM Uhr') — grouped by url for dates. The ?category= query param is ignored server-side (filtering is client-side on the categories field). No per-production detail endpoint, so sung cast (client-rendered) is left empty. Future-only → Wikidata backfill.

name: Oper Dortmund
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Opera division of Theater Dortmund; TYPO3, server-rendered. The shared /kalender/ lists event tiles with an 'event__division' (filter to 'Oper') + link /produktionen/detail/{slug}/. Detail page: h1 title (sometimes a programme-brand prefix like 'We DO Opera:'), composer in the subtitle after the h1 ('… von Composer'), creative team as 'Label Name' pairs, performance dates as 'für DD.MM.YYYY' lightbox labels. Per-night cast is JS-loaded (eventcast lightbox) so cast is left empty for now. Coverage = calendar window; deep history via Wikidata.

name: Staatstheater Nürnberg
notes:
Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Contentful-backed but server-rendered. Season page /spielzeit-{YY-YY}?sparte=Oper lists the season's opera productions, linked as spielplan-{YY-YY}/{slug}/{DD-MM-YYYY}/{HHMM} (date+time in the URL). The adapter walks the current + next season. Any such link renders the production detail: h1 title (soft-hyphenated), composer in ('… die Oper von Composer'), creative team as '

Label

Name

' pairs, sung cast as '

Name

Role, description

' pairs (institutional Orchester/Chor/Statisterie dropped; per-day 'Tagesaktuelles Dirigat' folded into conductor). All performance dates are date-stamped links on the detail page.

name: Aalto-Theater Essen
notes: Adapter implemented via shared houses/_schedule-cms.ts (future + wikidata backfill). The Aalto-Theater is the opera house of Theater und Philharmonie Essen. Server-rendered schedule under /programm/: '.performance' cards '<div class="performance … js-schedule-element" id="{YYYY-MM-DD}-p{prodId}">' with a performance__category, a startDate microdata time, and a link /programm/kalender/{slug}/{id}/. The link id is per-PERFORMANCE, so group by slug. Opera = category 'Aalto Musiktheater' — but that bucket also holds guided tours and children's concerts, so the adapter keeps only cards whose detail '<meta description>' names a composer ('… von X' before 'Besetzung:'). Detail meta is the same Title/Composer/Besetzung run as the other CMS houses (reuses parseMeta). Coverage = server-rendered window; deep history via Wikidata.

name: Hessisches Staatstheater Wiesbaden
notes: Adapter implemented via shared houses/_schedule-cms.ts (future + wikidata backfill). Same schedule-card CMS as Aalto Essen, but the schedule lives under /spielplan/ and opera cards carry the category 'Musiktheater' (categories have a trailing colon). Link id is per-performance → group by slug; non-opera cards filtered by requiring a 'von Composer' in the detail meta. Coverage = server-rendered window; deep history via Wikidata.

name: Wiener Staatsoper
notes: Excellent archive: the Aufführungsdatenbank covers performances back to 1869. Modern spielplan emits structured JSON; check /en/play-schedule and the backend calendar API.

name: Badisches Staatstheater Karlsruhe
notes:
Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). NOTE the host: the hyphenated www.staatstheater-karlsruhe.de works fine; the karlsruhe.de subdomain (staatstheater.karlsruhe.de) hard-403s — earlier 'blocked' verdict was the wrong domain. Server-rendered. Opera season list /programm/oper/spielplan/ links each production as programm/info/{id}/. Detail page: h1 title, '

{genre} von {Composer}

' subtitle, a Besetzung of '' rows (creative + sung cast, German labels; institutional rows dropped), and a 'Termine' section of '{Weekday}, {DD.MM.}, {HH:MM}' (year omitted → inferred from jquery_calendar_start_date season anchor). Future-only → Wikidata backfill.
LabelName

name: Staatstheater Augsburg
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Vue SPA over a public CouchDB view: _design/spieltermine/_view/by_datum_public_2?include_docs=true&startkey="YYYY-MM-DD" returns one doc per performance (sorte=spieltermin) with thea_genre (filter Oper/Operette/Musiktheater), thea_titel, thea_autor (composer), beginn (ISO), thea_link (slug), thea_ort. Grouped by slug.

name: Anhaltisches Theater Dessau
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Vite SPA over a custom JSON API: /api/termine returns a flat array of all performances with genre (keep Oper/Operette/Musical), titel, stueck_l1/stueck_l2 (form+composer lines → composer from 'von X'), beginn (ISO), ort, stueck_kuerzel (slug). Grouped by slug.

name: Theater Freiburg
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). cb-event house; spielplan JS-rendered but backed by /de_DE/event.json?m=calendar_events&fields[category]=musictheater&p=N → {Results:[{Events:[…]}],IsLastPage}. Each Event: Title, DateTimeAtom (ISO), TimeLocation (time+venue), OpusInfoShort ('Form von Composer'), Slug (/de_DE/programm/{slug}.{id}). Grouped by slug. (Plain event.json without a fields[category] returns 500.)

name: Staatstheater Mainz
notes:
Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Kirby CMS, server-rendered. Opera section /veranstaltungen/oper-{YY-YY} (walk current+next season) links productions as /veranstaltungen/oper-{YY-YY}/{slug}. Detail: '
{Title} von {Composer} ({year})
' (title before ' von ', composer via composerFromText), TERMINE plain DD.MM.YYYY dates (since-floored to drop stray historic refs).

name: Saarländisches Staatstheater
notes: PARKED: adapter implemented but /detail/ pages 403 in bursts even through the proxy's residential IP — an anti-bot rate-limit trips after the first few of the ~74 detail fetches (the /produktionen index is fine). Needs a gentler approach (per-request delays / a session cookie / render mode) or a different data source. TYPO3, server-rendered. /produktionen links every production as /detail/{slug} (all divisions; sparte filter is client-side). Fetch each detail, keep those whose <title> is 'Title | Musiktheater | …'; h1 title, subtitle 'Oper/Operette von Composer' (composerFromText), Termine dates as plain DD.MM.YYYY. ~74 detail fetches (filtered to opera).

name: Staatstheater Kassel
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Nuxt SPA over a public Strapi GraphQL backend (backend.staatstheater-kassel.de/graphql; the HTML frontend is an empty shell). POST an 'events' query joining Play→Production; filter Play.Section_1=='Musiktheater'; composer=Production.Writer; date=Event.StartDate (UTC ISO). Grouped by Play.Slug.

name: Mainfranken Theater Würzburg
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). spiritec WebCMS, server-rendered. /programm/spielplan/ '.performance' cards (id={YYYY-MM-DD}-p{id}, data-day-token, schema.org startDate) link /programm/spielplan/{slug}/{id}/; card id is per-performance → group by slug. Detail <meta description> is the shared 'Title, Oper von Composer, Besetzung: Role: Name' run → keep opera genres + reuse parseMeta for composer+cast.

name: Theater Erfurt
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Craft CMS, server-rendered. Opera index /programm/musiktheater links every opera as /stuecke/{slug}. Each detail page: h1 title, composer in '… Musik von X …' subtitle (markup concatenates without spaces, so composerFromText cuts at Libretto/Regie/etc.), dates as <time datetime=YYYY-MM-DD> in .dates-cards (time-of-day not in the date attr). Enumerate slugs from the index, fetch each detail.

name: Staatstheater Darmstadt
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Custom webfactory CMS, server-rendered. /spielplan/ '<article class=termin>' rows: termin__anchor → /veranstaltungen/{slug}.{id}/, <h3 class=termin__title>, <time datetime> (ISO), venue span, termin__details first line = genre+composer ('Oper von X'). Keep opera genres (Oper/Operette/Kammeroper/Familienoper/Musiktheater/Kurzopern); composer via composerFromText. Grouped by slug; windowed coverage.

name: Oper Halle
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Django CMS, server-rendered. /de/program?genre=oper filters to opera server-side. Each '<li class=event-item>': title <a class=event-title><span class=h2>, date as plain text 'Mi, 10.06.2026, 19:30 Uhr', location, link /de/program/{slug}/{id} (one per performance). Grouped by slug; composer fetched from the detail page subtitle ('… von X').

name: Staatstheater Cottbus
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Custom 'visioncontent' CMS, server-rendered. /de/spielplan.html: one performance row per '<div id=article_{slug} class=event-table>' with fulldate (ISO), event-time, event-location, event-division (genre — keep Musiktheater; the ?rubrik= param does NOT filter server-side), title <h3 class=title>, composer in <div class=subtitle> ('… von X'). Grouped by slug; parsed by splitting on the article marker.

name: Oper Chemnitz
notes: Adapter implemented (future + wikidata backfill). Opera house of Die Theater Chemnitz; server-rendered. /spielplan/ lists one '<section class=cc_event>' per performance: cc_hllink → /spielplan/detailseite/{slug}/{id}, title <h2>, composer in <h3> 'genre von X', genre in <div class=cc_type> (keep Musiktheater/Oper/Operette/Zeitgenössische Oper; require a composer to drop Führungen), date/time/venue in cc_date/cc_time/cc_loc. Grouped by slug. Cast not in the listing. Coverage = ~current-month window; deep history via Wikidata.

name: Staatstheater am Gärtnerplatz
notes:
Adapter implemented (render + wikidata backfill). Munich's second opera house. NOTE proxy:true — gaertnerplatztheater.de refuses the CI datacenter IP ('Unable to connect'), so the detail-page fetchHtml calls must go through the proxy (the spielplan render already does via fetchRendered). The spielplan /de/spielplan/index.html is JS-rendered (fetchRendered → proxy stealth render). Each performance is a '
' linking to /de/produktionen/{slug}.html; sparte- class = genre (14=Oper, 2=Operette kept; 3=Musical, 4=Ballett, 5=Konzert, 33=Opernstudio dropped). One render gives the full season's dates (grouped by slug). Each server-rendered detail page: h1 title, composer ('… Musik von X Libretto …'), Besetzung as 'function_person' rows (LabelName / …>; creative + sung cast, German labels incl. 'Dirigat'). Future-only → Wikidata backfill.

name: Bayerische Staatsoper
notes:
Adapter implemented (live render + Wikipedia + Wikidata). staatsoper.de serves a JS challenge and an 'EIN HOFFENTLICH KURZES INTERMEZZO!' bot-fallback to anything that looks headless — but a STEALTH headless render (renderHtml masks navigator.webdriver/window.chrome/plugins) gets the real page (verified residential + CI datacenter IP; the runner's google-chrome via CHROME_PATH). LIVE: render /spielplan, parse '.activity-list__row' (genre→Oper via data-schedule-filter; slug+date+time from the /stuecke/{slug}/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{HHMM}-{id} URL; title=, composer=toggle

, venue). Cast not in the listing. Coverage = rendered window (lazy-loads more on scroll — TODO if deeper needed). HISTORICAL premiere casts 2013/14–2017/18 from the Wikipedia 'Premierenbesetzungen ab 2014' list. Wikidata P1191 backfill. Historical premiere casts come from the Wikipedia list 'Premierenbesetzungen der Bayerischen Staatsoper ab 2014' (covers 2013/14–2017/18, 30 premieres): each is a colspan title row ('Work, … Musik von Composer / Composer (Musik) / von Librettist und Composer, DD. Monat YYYY') + a 4-column detail row (Dirigent·Chorleiter | Regie·Ausstattung·Licht | Sängerinnen | Sänger) of 'Name Role'. ~8 well-known works omit the composer in the source → null, filled by the cross-house merge. Wikidata (P1191) backfills further premieres.

name: Royal Opera House
notes: Tessitura-backed ticketing; production + performance JSON behind the what's-on pages. Collections/archive site (ROH Collections) covers historical productions.

name: Metropolitan Opera
notes: Archive importer IMPLEMENTED (backfill-only): houses/metropolitan-opera.ts walks archives.metopera.org season by season (search.jsp date_start/date_end, one response per season), groups performances into productions on the 'New Production' flag, and enriches each from record.jsp (cast + creative team + composer). Full 1883→today is a long one-off — bound with --since. Live current season (jsonld-event from metopera.org calendar JSON) is still TODO; incremental runs here are a no-op. enabled:false so it stays out of the nightly run.

name: Teatro alla Scala
notes: Archivio storico Ricordi + La Scala's own archive for past seasons. Live cartellone has structured event data.
